rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended component type or implementation scope while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in the component type annex and implementation-level bindings in the implementation annex
Use visible feature names in contracts when possible, avoiding subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for component-level contracts and implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume` and `guarantee` for behavioral contracts, `eq` for equations, and `assign` for output bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes and vice versa
irrelevant_topics: specific requirement items that should become clauses
